Size = crash volume · Click markers for details
Privacy: This page has no backend, no analytics, and no cookies. Nothing you type is stored or collected. Address search and commute routing are handled by OpenStreetMap Nominatim and OSRM (both open source). Your queries go directly to those services and are subject to their privacy policies.

11 — Pedestrians & Cyclists

The Most Vulnerable on Metro Streets

4109 pedestrian crashes and 2125 cyclist crashes across the metro in 2024.

Pedestrian Crashes by City
CHP CCRS 2024
4109
pedestrian crashes in 2024. Los Angeles accounts for 1,451 — 35% of the metro total despite having 58% of the population.
12 — Vision Zero LA

Vision Zero LA: Progress Toward Zero

Los Angeles Metro adopted Vision Zero LA in 2015, setting a target of 0 traffic fatalities by 2025. The baseline was 260 fatalities in 2015.

Fatalities: Goal vs. Actual
2015–2025
13 — Sources

Where This Data Comes From

CHP CCRS
California Highway Patrol publishes all crash records through the Crash Records System (CCRS) on data.ca.gov. 104,866 crashes in LA County in 2024, each geocoded with coordinates, severity, and behavioral flags from the Parties detail table.

CCRS Dataset on data.ca.gov
14 — Methodology

How I Built This

Raw CCRS crash records are queried from the CKAN DataStore API, filtered to County Code 19 (Los Angeles). Party-level data is joined by Collision ID to extract DUI/impairment status and speed limits. Records without valid coordinates are excluded. 104,866 records processed for 2024.

Data: CHP CCRS
Built for r/LosAngeles · Buy me a coffee
